mission.title: surrealdb: Feature: Add lightweight database backend for embedded use
mission.description: Feature: Add lightweight database backend for embedded use. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current surrealdb setup
steps.description:
Verify your surrealdb version and configuration:
```bash
surreal version
```
This feature requires a working surrealdb installation.

steps.title: Review surrealdb configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant surrealdb configuration:
### Is your feature request related to a problem?

I am using surrealdb as an embedded database for my application and currently the only backing database option is RocksDB. I'm running into the following problems:

- RocksDB is large.

steps.title: Use SurrealKV as lightweight embedded backend
steps.description:
SurrealDB ships with SurrealKV as its lightweight embedded storage engine, replacing the need for RocksDB in embedded use cases. To start SurrealDB with the embedded SurrealKV backend:
```bash
# Start with SurrealKV (file-based embedded storage)
surreal start --user root --pass root surrealkv://path/to/data.db

# Or use the memory backend for ephemeral data
surreal start --user root --pass root memory
```
SurrealKV provides a lightweight, pure-Rust embedded key-value store purpose-built for SurrealDB's transaction model. It replaced the earlier RocksDB dependency for embedded use cases and offers smaller binary sizes and simpler deployment.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Feature: Add lightweight database backend for embedded use" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: SurrealDB evaluated Sled as a lightweight embedded backend but determined it was incompatible due to Sled's transaction model limitations. SurrealDB instead built their own embedded storage engine (SurrealKV) which shipped in later versions, providing the lightweight embedded use case requested.

prerequisites.description: A working surrealdb installation or development environment.
